name: SillyTavern MCP Server
what_it_does: MCP server integrating with SillyTavern — the popular AI roleplay and character chat frontend. Enables AI agents to interact with SillyTavern's character management, conversation history, and LLM backends — bridging SillyTavern's rich roleplay ecosystem with MCP-based agent workflows.
use_cases: Accessing SillyTavern character cards and personas from AI agent workflows
Managing SillyTavern conversation history and chat logs from automation agents
Integrating SillyTavern's LLM backend connections into broader agent pipelines
Building AI workflows that leverage SillyTavern's character and context management
not_for: Production enterprise AI applications (SillyTavern is a personal/creative tool)
Teams without SillyTavern installed and configured
best_when: A developer or power user running SillyTavern for AI roleplay and creative writing wants to integrate SillyTavern's character management and conversation capabilities into broader AI agent workflows.
avoid_when: You don't use SillyTavern. Also: SillyTavern is primarily a personal creative tool — not suited for enterprise/production use cases.

auth.notes: No authentication — connects to local SillyTavern instance. SillyTavern must be running locally.

pricing.notes: SillyTavern is free open source. MCP server is free open source. LLM API costs from connected backends apply separately.

performance.rate_limits: Depends on connected LLM backend rate limits

known_agent_gotchas: Requires SillyTavern running locally — not a standalone service
SillyTavern API evolves frequently with new releases — check compatibility
Community MCP server — may lag behind SillyTavern updates
Character card format is SillyTavern-specific — not portable to other systems
agent_readiness.security_notes: Local access to SillyTavern. Chat logs may contain personal/sensitive roleplay content. No remote exposure by default.

security_score_components.security_notes: Local SillyTavern access. Chat data may be personal. No network exposure by default.
